items.title: Tabular classification and regression
items.description: Random forests are a reliable default for structured data—customer churn, credit scoring, pricing, and sensor readings—where feature scaling is optional and non-linear boundaries are common.

items.title: Feature importance screening
items.description: Mean decrease in impurity across trees gives a quick ranking of predictive features for exploratory analysis and stakeholder communication.

items.title: High-variance base learners stabilized by ensembling
items.description: When a single decision tree swings wildly across train/test splits, bagging many deep trees and averaging is a standard variance-reduction remedy.

items.title: Parallel training on multi-core CPUs
items.description: Independent trees map naturally to parallel workers (n_jobs in scikit-learn), making forests attractive when boosting's sequential loop is too slow.
